title: Minimum Score Triangulation of Polygon
hints: Consider the first triangle formed involving vertex 0. It splits the polygon into two smaller sub-polygons.
This structure suggests dynamic programming. Define dp[i][j] as the minimum score for the polygon from vertex i to j.
For each possible third vertex k between i and j, the cost is values[i]*values[j]*values[k] plus the optimal scores of the sub-polygons.
